overview_table.Features: Primal Battlecasting Ranger Enclave Channel Ancients
Tumbling Strike Acute Sense Ability Improvement
Elusive Tumble Piercing Sense Ability Improvement
Ancient Memories III Ranger Enclave Feature

features.name: Primal Battlecasting
features.description:
As a Ranger, you are able to prepare and cast spells from the Primal
spell list:

- **Spells Prepared.** At 1st Rank, you may prepare 4 spells for this
  Class at the end of each long rest. You may cast those prepared spells
  using this feature.

- **Class Circle Cap.** Each spell you prepare may have a maximum
  baseline Circle of 1st Circle.

- **Heightening.** When you cast spells using this feature, you may
  heighten spells up to your Class Circle Cap.

- **Spell Points.** At 1st Rank, you gain 2 Spell Points. When you cast
  spells using this feature, you must expend Spell Points equal to the
  Circle of the spell. Spell Points are regained at the end of each long
  rest.

- **Spellcasting Modifier.** Your spellcasting modifier when casting
  spells using this feature is WIS.

  - **Your Spell Attack Bonus** = your WIS + your Tier Bonus

  - **Your Spell Save DC** = 8 + your WIS + your Tier Bonus

  - **Spellcasting Checks** made with spells prepared for this class use
    your WIS.

- **Signature Spell.** Your designated signature spell may be one of the
  1st Circle or higher spells prepared for this feature.

- **Higher Ranks.** At higher Ranks, the spells prepared, Spell Points,
  and Class Circle Cap all improve as described in the Class Overview
  Table.

features.name: Ranger Enclave
features.flavor_text: You are the product of a millennium of lessons.
features.description:
At 1st Rank, you choose a Ranger Enclave to reflect the traditions and
skills you have honed with time and patience. You gain features from
your chosen Ranger Enclave at 1st, 4th, and 8th Rank.

features.name: Channel Ancients
features.flavor_text: Within your body blooms an unforgotten strength, inheriting the promises of the primal forces of the world. The will of the yearning wilds moves through your actions.
features.description:
At 1st Rank, as an action during combat, you may *channel* an *ancient*
that you know, gaining its listed benefits until the end of combat.

If you channeled an ancient on your last turn, you may take a free
action once during your current turn to either channel another ancient
that you know or *evoke* to take a free Move or Attack action.

You know four ancients: one provided by your Ranger Enclave and the
**Echoes**, **Permanence**, and **Ruin** ancients described below:

### Echoes

Ranger Ancient

Your Speed is increased by 10 feet.

### Permanence

Ranger Ancient

You gain your Half Guard AC.

### Ruin

Ranger Ancient

Your weapon attacks deal an additional **1d4 damage** on a hit. This
damage die becomes 2d4/3d4/4d4 at 4th/7th/10th Rank, respectively.

features.name: Tumble
features.flavor_text: Preternatural senses honed by many dangers allow for quick escapes.
features.description:
At 2nd Rank, as a reaction immediately before you are attacked, you may
impose disadvantage on the attack check. Afterwards, you may travel up
to 10 feet.

features.name: Ancient Memories I
features.flavor_text: Though the land holds a deep memory for any who would listen, the whisper is quiet.
features.description:
At 2nd Rank, select one Ancient Memory feature of your choosing from the
list below, which enhances one of your ancients in various ways. During
the Retrain downtime activity, you may redo the selection of all of your
chosen Ancient Memories.

## Memories of Echoes

### Domain

2nd Rank Ranger Ancient Memory (Echoes)

While your **Echoes** ancient is active, your movement ignores difficult
terrain.

### Dusk

2nd Rank Ranger Ancient Memory (Echoes)

While your **Echoes** ancient is active, when you take the Dodge action,
you may travel up to 10 feet.

### Rapids

2nd Rank Ranger Ancient Memory (Echoes)

While your **Echoes** ancient is active, you may add 5 feet to your
movement pool as a free action at the start of each of your turns.

## Memories of Permanence

### Ardor

2nd Rank Ranger Ancient Memory (Permanence)

While your **Permanence** ancient is active, you may **regain ST** equal
to 2 × your Ranger Rank as a free action at the start of each of your
turns.

### Balance

2nd Rank Ranger Ancient Memory (Permanence)

While your **Permanence** ancient is active, when you take the
Anticipate action, you gain advantage on the next saving throw you make
before the start of your next turn.

### Roots

2nd Rank Ranger Ancient Memory (Permanence)

While your **Permanence** ancient is active, when you would be forcibly
moved, you may prevent that movement as a free action. If you do, you
may also push the source of the effect 5 feet away from you.

## Memories of Ruin

### Collapse

2nd Rank Ranger Ancient Memory (Ruin)

While your **Ruin** ancient is active, you gain advantage on all attack
checks against any creature that has a fully depleted DB.

### Fangs

2nd Rank Ranger Ancient Memory (Ruin)

While your **Ruin** ancient is active, the critical hit threshold of all
attacks you make are reduced by 1.

### Trail

2nd Rank Ranger Ancient Memory (Ruin)

While your **Ruin** ancient is active, you gain advantage on Search
checks against creatures that took damage from you during this combat.

features.name: Tumbling Strike
features.flavor_text: Survival is secured in the narrow slivers of opportunity.
features.description:
At 3rd Rank, after each time you use **Tumble**, you may make a weapon
attack.

features.name: Acute Sense
features.flavor_text: Predators can find prey making the smallest movement, and your quarry's breath is caught by your ears.
features.description:
At 3rd Rank, when you succeed on a Search check, the target becomes
**perceived** by you and your initiative partner.

features.name: Ability Improvement
features.description:
At 3rd, 6th, and 9th Rank, you gain an Ability Improvement. Each time
you gain an Ability Improvement, distribute three permanent +1 increases
to your ability modifiers. You may increase the same ability modifier
multiple times.

The maximum ability modifier that may be achieved is limited by your
character's Level. The maximum is +5 at Level 3, increases to +6 at
Level 6, and finally becomes +7 at Level 9.

features.name: Exploration
features.description:
At 4th, 7th, and 10th Rank, you gain an Exploration with Ranger being
the exploring Class. Each time you gain an Exploration, you gain the
following benefit:

- **Bonus Rank.** You choose any Class other than Ranger and gain one
  Rank in the chosen Class.

features.name: Ranger Enclave Feature
features.description: At 4th Rank, you gain a feature for your Ranger Enclave.

features.name: Ancient Memories II
features.flavor_text: You are propelled in your search for the truths the land will teach you: the lessons carved into the stones of the earth.
features.description:
At 5th Rank, you gain an additional Ancient Memory feature chosen from
those listed at 2nd Rank. The Ancient Memory feature you choose must
enhance a different ancient than one you have already enhanced. During
the Retrain downtime activity, you may redo the selection of all of your
chosen Ancient Memories.

features.name: Elusive Tumble
features.flavor_text: Avoid that which cannot be weathered, and weather that which cannot be avoided.
features.description:
At 6th Rank, you may use **Tumble** as a reaction immediately before you
make a **Reflex** saving throw instead of as a reaction to being
attacked. If you do, you gain advantage on the saving throw, and all
other effects of **Tumble** occur.

features.name: Piercing Sense
features.flavor_text: Survival is not vindictive; purpose, not malice, guides your lethal arrows.
features.description:
At 6th Rank, once per turn after succeeding on a Search check, you may
make a weapon attack as a free action against the target of the Search
check.

features.name: Ability Improvement
features.description:
At 6th Rank, you gain an Ability Improvement. Each time you gain an
Ability Improvement, distribute three permanent +1 increases to your
ability modifiers. You may increase the same ability modifier multiple
times.

The maximum ability modifier that may be achieved is limited by your
character's Level. The maximum is +5 at Level 3, increases to +6 at
Level 6, and finally becomes +7 at Level 9.

features.name: Adaptation I
features.flavor_text: Those who do not change, die.
features.description:
At 7th Rank, you choose an **elemental**, **biological**, or
**supernatural** damage type and gain the following benefits:

- **Damage Buffer.** You gain DB 15 against the chosen damage type.

- **Damage Conversion.** When you hit with a weapon attack, you may
  convert all of the damage dealt to the chosen damage type.

During the Retrain downtime activity, you may redo your selection of
chosen damage type.

features.name: Exploration
features.description:
At 7th Rank, you gain an Exploration with Ranger being the exploring
Class. Each time you gain an Exploration, you gain the following
benefit:

- **Bonus Rank.** You choose any Class other than Ranger and gain one
  Rank in the chosen Class.

features.name: Ancient Memories III
features.flavor_text: Internalized lessons now guide your path. Observing your fellow mortals in their mundane aspirations, you see the inevitability of all life. Death, renewal, and rejuvenation.
features.description:
At 8th Rank, select an additional Ancient Memory feature chosen from
those listed at 2nd Rank. The Ancient Memory feature you choose must
enhance a different ancient than one you have already enhanced. During
the Retrain downtime activity, you may redo the selection of all of your
chosen Ancient Memories.

features.name: Ranger Enclave Feature
features.description: At 8th Rank, you gain a feature for your Ranger Enclave.

features.name: Improved Tumble
features.flavor_text: The honed reflexes of a being of pure intuition are unmatched.
features.description:
At 9th Rank, the distance you travel when you use **Tumble** becomes 20
feet. You gain advantage on weapon attacks made as part of **Tumbling
Strike**.

features.name: Ability Improvement
features.description:
At 9th Rank, you gain an Ability Improvement. Each time you gain an
Ability Improvement, distribute three permanent +1 increases to your
ability modifiers. You may increase the same ability modifier multiple
times.

The maximum ability modifier that may be achieved is limited by your
character's Level. The maximum is +5 at Level 3, increases to +6 at
Level 6, and finally becomes +7 at Level 9.

features.name: Adaptation II
features.flavor_text: All play their part in the time they are given, though some overcome enough challenges to become the evolutionary force.
features.description:
At 10th Rank, you may choose a second **elemental**, **biological**, or
**supernatural** damage type:

- **Damage Buffer.** Your **Adaptation** DB becomes 20 and applies to
  both of your chosen damage types.

- **Damage Conversion.** When you hit with a weapon attack, you may
  convert all of the damage dealt to either of the chosen damage types.

During the Retrain downtime activity, you may redo your selection of
both chosen damage types.

features.name: Exploration
features.description:
At 10th Rank, you gain an Exploration with Ranger being the exploring
Class. Each time you gain an Exploration, you gain the following
benefit:

- **Bonus Rank.** You choose any Class other than Ranger and gain one
  Rank in the chosen Class.

features.name: Tumbling Spell
features.flavor_text: Instinctual magic conjured as easily as breath.
features.description:
At 11th Rank, when you use **Tumbling Strike**, you may forgo making the
attack to cast a spell instead with a casting time of 1 action using any
feature you have as a free action.

features.name: Ancient Recollection
features.flavor_text: Setting aside your immaterial ambitions, you embrace the full understanding of the natural order.
features.description:
At 11th Rank, select one of the following Ancient Memory features from
the list below or one listed at an earlier Rank. During the Retrain
downtime activity, you may redo the selection of all of your chosen
Ancient Memories.

### Currents

11th Rank Ranger Ancient Memory (Echoes)

While your **Echoes** ancient is active, the movement cost to travel is
halved when using any mobility.

### Mountain

11th Rank Ranger Ancient Memory (Permanence)

While your **Permanence** ancient is active, when the
incapacitated, stunned, or
frozen conditions are applied to you, you may prevent the
conditions from being applied to you. If you do, all of your ancients
cease being active.

### Eruption

11th Rank Ranger Ancient Memory (Ruin)

While your **Ruin** ancient is active, as a free action during each of
your turns, you may deal **4d4 damage** to any number of targets within
30 feet of you. The damage type dealt is one of the damage types chosen
for your **Adaptation** feature (you pick which).

features.name: Primal Conduit
features.flavor_text: Mortals spend their brief life boasting of their flickering candles under the starry expanse. But the battering winds topple their fortresses, the tremors in the earth sunder their foundations, and the rain erodes their monuments leaving not even a memory.
features.description:
At 12th Rank, as a free action once during each of your turns while you
have four or more ancients active at once, you may travel up to your
Speed, cleanse yourself of all negative conditions and effects, then
make a weapon attack. The weapon attack check gains advantage.

features.name: Final Reach
features.description:
When you reach character Level 12, you compute your *Total Rank*, which
is calculated as:

###### The sum of all of your Class Ranks

If your Total Rank is less than 16 when you reach Level 12, then for
each deficient Rank, you may choose one of the benefits below to modify
a feature granted by one of your Class Ranks:

- **Maneuvers.** You learn two additional maneuvers.

- **Battlecasting.** You gain a maneuver, an additional Spell Point, and
  an additional prepared spell for that Class.

- **Spellcasting.** You gain three additional Spell Points and three
  additional prepared spells for that Class.

Ranger Enclaves

features.name: Ranger Enclave: Dune Strider
features.flavor_text: The rumbling of an insatiable appetite slumbers beyond the earthen facade of the soil upon which you stand. Your nerves twinge in tune with every quake and tremor, a wholeness achieved through joining in with this bottomless thirst. The land will swallow the unready and reform anew.
features.description:
## Sand Traps

*Call into question the firm ground upon which they stand. Seize the
earth and drown them in it.*

At 1st Rank, as an action, you may create a *sand trap* at a target
floor within 30 feet of you. At the start of each of your turns, you may
have sand traps of your choosing cause all creatures of Size Large or
smaller within 5 feet of them to become stuck. Afterwards,
the chosen sand traps expire. This stuck condition has the
**Recoverable** condition trait, allowing an action to end the effect.

## Ancient of Cavitation

*The soft soils grant you their passage. With it, even the lifeless
earth is given new purpose.*

At 1st Rank, you learn the **Cavitation** ancient described below.

### Cavitation

Dune Strider Ranger Ancient

You gain basic Burrowing mobility. Whenever you travel while burrowing
within 5 feet of a floor, you may create a sand trap on that surface.

## Dustwhirl Tumble

*Eat my dust before it eats you.*

At 4th Rank, when you use **Tumble**, you may create a sand trap at your
location immediately before traveling.

## Sandstorm Torrent

*Scorched grounds and eroded cliffs are but prophecies etched by the
hands of time. Sinking ever further into the earth, such words alter the
foundations.*

At 8th Rank, you gain the following benefits:

- **Force of Erosion.** Once per turn while your **Cavitation** ancient
  is active, immediately before you make an attack check against a
  creature stuck from one of your sand traps, you may gain
  advantage on the attack check.

- **Shifting Sands.** The range at which your sand traps causes
  creatures to become stuck becomes 10 feet. When one of
  your sand traps causes a creature to become stuck, you
  may pull that creature to that sand trap.

features.name: Ranger Enclave: Nyctohawk
features.flavor_text: With legs carried by moonlit gales and the long shadows worn as skin, the very night itself becomes your cloak. Ever elusive, you hang in the corner of their eye and pounce when the moment is ripe. In one breath, a gliding shadow, a falling blade, and a lethal strike bears silent witness to your mark's final moments.
features.description:
## Umbral Focus

*At the moment to strike, your eyes narrow upon an unwary target. Their
luck ends tonight.*

At 1st Rank, at the start of each of your turns, you apply your *umbral
focus* to a target hostile creature that does not **perceive** you. Your
umbral focus expires at the end of each turn. Weapon attacks you make
against the creature with your umbral focus have their critical hit
threshold reduced by 1.

## Ancient of Predation

*The bird of prey tucks its wings as it dives towards its next meal.*

At 1st Rank, you learn the **Predation** ancient described below.

### Predation

Nyctohawk Ranger Ancient

You gain basic Jumping mobility. When you hit the creature with your
umbral focus with a weapon attack that was made during or immediately
after falling further than 20 feet, the target becomes
frightened of you until the start of your next turn.

## Mooncloak Tumble

*By the time they caught onto you, you were already gone.*

At 4th Rank, once per turn, when you use **Tumble**, you may take a free
Vanish action.

## Nocturnal Shroud

*A midnight mist drips from your form, hanging in the air like a thick
fog that smothers your foes.*

At 8th Rank, you gain the following benefits:

- **Into the Night.** While your **Predation** ancient is active, you
  gain advantage on Vanish checks.

- **Swallowing Shadow.** When you score a critical hit against the
  creature with your umbral focus, you may cause them to become
  disoriented until the end of your next turn.

features.name: Ranger Enclave: Wind Warden
features.flavor_text: Looking out over the great expanses, your heart flutters with wondrous freedom knowing there is no horizon beyond which the winds cannot carry you. Adrift within the art of the sky, you embody the dreamy separation of the material from its gravity. Though surrounded by thunderous tempest and billowing gales, your eyes remain ever forward and your soul forever at ease.
features.description:
## Blow Through

*Through graceful, fluid motions, your hand guides the air into sweeping
whirlwinds.*

At 1st Rank, when you deal damage with a weapon attack against a
creature, you may evenly divide the damage dealt between the target and
another target creature within 5 feet of them.

## Ancient of Galeforce

*Rippling winds spiral around you and rest at your feet. The air itself
springs forth on your behalf.*

At 1st Rank, you learn the **Galeforce** ancient described below.

### Galeforce

Wind Warden Ranger Ancient

As a free action, you may spend up to 15 feet of movement to cause a
target object or creature within 15 feet of you to make a **Poise**
saving throw against your Ranger Spell Save DC. On a failure, the target
is forcibly moved a distance equal to the movement spent in a direction
of your choosing.

## Blast Back Tumble

*The arrow's fletching catches in the hidden currents surrounding you.
Its whirling flight is renewed with winged memories.*

At 4th Rank, when you use **Tumble** followed by **Tumbling Strike**, if
the attack made against you was ranged and it missed, you may add its
damage roll to the damage roll of the **Tumbling Strike** weapon attack.

## Zephyr Form

*Once carried by the wind, it now flows through you.*

At 8th Rank, you gain the following benefits:

- **Cyclone Strike.** When you use **Blow Through**, you may now evenly
  divide the damage dealt between the target of the attack and up to two
  other target creatures within 5 feet of them.

- **Windwalk.** While your **Galeforce** ancient is active, but only
  during your turn, you gain basic Flying mobility.
